name: Pipe Flow
summary: A transparent Cesium water-pipe route effect with shell glow, internal flow, pressure waves, bubbles, and rounded bends.
useCases: Transparent water-pipe or utility-network flow visualization
Pump station, pipeline pressure, and drainage dispatch dashboards
Command-center route demos that need a more physical flow than simple light trails

positions.description: WGS84 pipe route points. Each point supports longitude, latitude, and optional height.

color.description: CSS color used by the pipe shell, water core, pressure waves, and bubbles.

speed.description: Flow speed multiplier for water movement, pressure waves, and bubbles.

width.description: Water core width in screen pixels. The transparent shell and highlights are layered wider around it.

pipeOpacity.description: Opacity of the transparent outer pipe shell.

waterOpacity.description: Opacity of the animated internal water core and pressure waves.

cornerRadius.description: Corner rounding strength for pipe bends. 0 keeps hard elbows; higher values insert smooth bend samples.

bubbleDensity.description: Number of animated bubble streaks inside the pipe. 0 disables bubble entities.

minimal.code:
import { createPipeFlowEffect } from '@ztgkzhaohao/geo-effect-kit'

const pipe = createPipeFlowEffect(viewer, {
  positions: [
    { longitude: 116.285, latitude: 39.87 },
    { longitude: 116.335, latitude: 39.92 },
    { longitude: 116.394, latitude: 39.91 },
    { longitude: 116.452, latitude: 39.95 },
    { longitude: 116.505, latitude: 39.9 },
  ],
  color: '#45dfff',
  speed: 1.35,
  width: 14,
  pipeOpacity: 0.34,
  waterOpacity: 0.88,
  cornerRadius: 0.22,
  bubbleDensity: 8,
})

pipe.flyTo()
pipe.destroy()

update.code:
pipe.update({
  color: '#73f7ff',
  speed: 2.1,
  width: 16,
  pipeOpacity: 0.42,
  waterOpacity: 0.92,
  cornerRadius: 0.28,
  bubbleDensity: 12,
})

notes: Pass an existing Cesium Viewer; the SDK does not create or own the viewer.
The effect is built from layered Cesium polylines: transparent pipe shell, white rim highlight, water core, pressure waves, and moving bubble streaks.
Changing positions, clampToGround, cornerRadius, or bubbleDensity rebuilds route entities; color, speed, width, and opacities update live.
Call destroy() during page cleanup to remove the data source and render loop.
